annotation.summary: Non-deterministic html-canonical divergence, PRE-EXISTING and unrelated to the openxml faithful-writer work (html reader/writer + html-canonical normalizer untouched). The fixture flips between canonical-equal and divergent across identical-code runs: when divergent, native emits ~837 extra bytes around the document's Open Graph <meta property="…"> tags (e.g. article:publisher) in word/head reconstruction. The difference is document metadata only — it renders identically — so it is cosmetic. The non-determinism (native html-writer head/meta emission ordering) is a separate test-stability issue to investigate; annotated here so the dashboard documents it whichever way the flake lands.
